text: The aim of this study is to determine the impact of technology devices used by high school students in Denizli, which is located in the southwestern part of Turkey, on high school seniors’ academic achievements. In the research, parametric test methods were used to evaluate the relations between students’ academic achievements, which were evaluated by scores obtained from the national university entrance exams (AHE), and mobile phone ownership, mobile internet, computer ownership at home, internet availability at home and the number of television sets in the households. The results obtained from the research revealed that having a computer at home and internet availability at home are predictor variables that have positive effects on the academic achievements of students. On the other hand, mobile phone ownership and the number of television sets at home are not statistical variables that affect students’ academic achievements. Keywords: Academic achievements, technology devices, high school students The findings of the study show that some digital equipment such as learning how to access the internet or self-learning can facilitate access to knowledge, but it does not always have an impact on academic achievements of students, depending on the devices used and the conditions they are used in.
